explanations.4: This calls a helper function to verify that both 'fzf' and 'docker' tools are installed; if either is missing, the script exits early.
explanations.5: This checks if there are any running containers; if there aren't any, the script exits without doing anything.
explanations.39: This extracts just the first line of fzf's output, which tells us which key the user pressed (Enter for stop, or Ctrl-K for kill).
explanations.41: This takes all lines after the first one from fzf's output and uses awk to grab only the first column (the container IDs), discarding the other columns.
explanations.46: The `(@f)` syntax splits the selection string by newlines into separate array elements, so each container ID becomes its own item in the array.
explanations.59: The `${target_ids[*]}` joins all container IDs into a single space-separated string for display; this is different from line 60 which uses `[@]` to pass them as separate arguments.
explanations.60: The `eval` command executes the docker command stored in the variable; we use `[@]` here to pass each container ID as a separate argument to docker.
